summary_text:
S:
The patient reports back pain for approximately one week, with occasional relief and recurrence. He attempted over-the-counter medications without sustained relief. He describes a twisting injury approximately one week prior to the onset of pain, and reports occasional left eye irritation coinciding with the back pain. He reports occasional visits to the bakery, consuming sweets, and acknowledges this intake is monitored. He reports checking his blood sugar in the mornings most days, but occasionally skips this practice, and occasionally goes skiing. His blood sugars have trended towards the higher side when he does check them. He takes his metformin twice daily.

O:
Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation over the lumbar spine, particularly at the central region. Range of motion is limited. Neurological exam is intact. Lung sounds are clear. Heart rhythm is regular. Blood pressure is slightly low. No edema is noted. Glucose levels have been elevated recently.

A:
The patient's presentation is consistent with a musculoskeletal low back strain, likely secondary to a recent injury. His diabetes is currently under control, but requires ongoing monitoring.

P:
Order a low back x-ray for outpatient evaluation. Order physical therapy twice per week to address pain and strengthen back muscles. Prescribe ibuprofen 400 mg twice daily as needed with tylenol up to three times daily. Prescribe flexeril 30 mg at bedtime as needed for muscle relaxation. Order an A1C test to assess diabetes control. Schedule a follow-up in 4-6 weeks to review x-ray results and assess response to physical therapy. Advise patient to monitor blood sugars daily and keep a record. Advise patient to limit carbohydrate intake, particularly from bakery items.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Back pain.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports history of diabetes.

SOCIAL HISTORY

The patient's family owns and runs a bakery.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports taking metformin twice daily.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Musculoskeletal: Reports back pain.
Neurological: Denies numbness or tingling in feet.

PHYSICAL EXAM

Eyes
- Examination: No redness, discharge, or icterus.

Ears, Nose, Mouth, and Throat
- Examination of Ears: Auditory canal and tympanic membranes are clear.

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: Clear bilaterally.

Cardiovascular
- Auscultation of Heart: Regular rate and rhythm.
Musculoskeletal
- Examination: No pain to palpation of the upper back. Pain to palpation of the midline of the lower back, especially on the left. No pain to palpation on the right lower back. Mildly positive straight leg rise on the right Positive straight leg rise on the left.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Low back pain.
- Medical Reasoning: I believe he has a musculoskeletal low back strain. I do not expect to see anything significant on x-ray, but we will obtain one for further evaluation of any possible disc issues.
- Patient Education and Counseling: We discussed his diagnosis at length today. I explained that physical therapy can help with the pain, but also strengthen some of the muscles in the back. I explained that he needs to be careful while taking medications, such as an overuse of muscle relaxers if he is doing any strenuous or hazardous activities such as driving.
- Medical Treatment: Low back x-ray ordered. Referral to physical therapy provided. He will take 400 mg of ibuprofen up to 3 times per day. Tylenol can be used for severe pain. Prescription for Flexeril also provided.

2. Diabetes.
- Medical Reasoning: His diabetes is currently under control.
- Patient Education and Counseling: We discussed the importance of maintaining a healthy lifestyle. I encouraged him to try an avoid foods high in sugar. He asked many questions and I feel he is confident in the plan at this point.
- Medical Treatment: Hemoglobin A1c ordered as he has not had an updated one in almost 5 months. He will continue with his diet and exercise. Continue current dose of metformin for the time being. The patient will be checking his blood sugar in the morning and will log this information for me.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: Glucose levels have been elevated recently.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The note does not specify that this is from a clinician-measured lab or point-of-care test; in context it aligns more with the patient's reported home blood sugar trends, which belong in the Subjective section. However, it could also be interpreted as a summarized objective trend, so the miscategorization is not clearly major.

section_miscategorizations.text: His diabetes is currently under control, but requires ongoing monitoring.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an appropriate clinician assessment statement summarizing the status of a chronic condition and the need for monitoring; it is correctly placed in the Assessment section.
